title: The Hengwrt MS of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
summary: The General Corpus XML is a repository copy of University of Michigan CME item AGZ8233.0001.001, an uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding linked to 804 historical page images. The electronic item represents Frederick J. Furnivall's six-part 1868-1879 Hengwrt edition, whose parts were issued from 1869 through 1881. The edition principally transcribes National Library of Wales Peniarth MS 392D (Hengwrt 154), but adds four Wife-Preamble passages from Cambridge Dd.4.24, restores a Monk-Nun's Priest link from Royal MS 18 C II and Harley MS 1758, cites two French Melibee comparisons from Royal MSS 19 C XI and 19 C VII, and supplies the lost end of the Parson's Tale and the Retraction from Christ Church MS 152 and British Library Add MS 5140. Its appendix draws Gamelyn and three links from further British Library witnesses, restores the Canon's Yeoman sequence principally from the Lichfield Cathedral manuscript Furnivall called MS 2 (currently reported as MS 29) with two Royal MS 18 C II supplements, and reproduces twenty-three Ellesmere teller portraits as wood engravings. Complete provider-published Hengwrt, Dd.4.24, Royal 19 C VII, and Ellesmere IIIF objects and a complete bound-copy scan of Furnivall's edition are cached locally with exact work portions; unavailable and request-only source routes remain explicit.
review_notes: The cataloged subject is Furnivall's composite edition-level compilation, not the abstract Canterbury Tales and not a diplomatic representation of one witness throughout.

The source edition's intellectual sequence and the checked bound scan's carrier sequence differ: the scan inserts Gamelyn after main p. 128, while the U-M encoding serializes the appendices after main p. 654. This is modeled as carrier arrangement, not a different textual lineage.
The 804 U-M PB sequence is complete and sequential in the repository XML, but automated retrieval of a linked page image returned HTTP 403 and no stable complete image bundle was verified.
Exact passage scopes are recorded for every supplemental witness. A witness named in one appendix does not become a source for the whole edition.

Copyright in medieval text, nineteenth-century editorial or engraved matter, XML markup, and modern digital images is recorded in separate layers. Public access or local caching is not treated as an open reuse license.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for agz8233
entities.description: The exact CME XML artifact used by General Corpus.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item AGZ8233.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 uncorrected OCR encoding at TEI Libraries Level 1, with omitted matter marked and historical page images linked by the item interface.

entities.label: University of Michigan 804-page historical image sequence for AGZ8233.0001.001
entities.description: The page-image layer linked from the U-M item and represented by 804 sequential PB references in the XML. Its current direct-image access and downloadable completeness remain unresolved.

entities.label: Furnivall's Hengwrt MS of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales
entities.description: A composite manuscript edition issued for the Chaucer Society in six parts. It prints the Hengwrt order and readings where extant, then supplies identified gaps, alternative links, Gamelyn, the Canon's Yeoman sequence, and teller portraits from other sources.

date_statements.label: part issue range
date_statements.notes: Cataloged as Chaucer Society First Series nos. 3, 9, 27, 39, 51, and 71.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Hengwrt MS of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, ed. Frederick J. Furnivall, Chaucer Society First Series 3, 9, 27, 39, 51, 71 (London: Chaucer Society / N. Trubner, 1868-1879; six parts issued 1869-1881).
bibliographic.title: The Hengwrt MS of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales

physical_description.format: six parts, commonly bound as one volume

entities.label: Wikimedia Commons / Internet Archive complete bound-copy scan of Furnivall's Hengwrt edition
entities.description: An 800-page PDF scan of a complete bound copy. The scan's binding inserts Gamelyn after main p. 128 and places the other appendix matter and plates after the main sequence.

entities.label: National Library of Wales Peniarth MS 392D (Hengwrt Chaucer)
entities.description: The principal manuscript witness for Furnivall's edition: an early, incomplete Canterbury Tales codex whose original parchment leaves occupy ff. 2-250.

physical_description.support: parchment, with later leaves and binding matter

entities.label: National Library of Wales complete 518-canvas IIIF presentation of Peniarth MS 392D
entities.description: The complete provider-published digital object, including covers, boards, old-binding leaf, manuscript leaves, edges, and a 3D view.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with JPEG images

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.4.24
entities.description: A first-quarter fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript used for four discrete additions in Furnivall's Wife of Bath's Preamble: III.575-584, 609-612, 619-626, and 717-720.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: iv + 180 + iv leaves; target additions on ff. 73v, 74r, and 75r

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library complete 377-canvas IIIF presentation of MS Dd.4.24
entities.description: The complete provider-published CUDL object. The four Wife-Preamble additions occupy three discontiguous leaves: f. 73v/canvas 114, f. 74r/canvas 115, and f. 75r/canvas 117.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with JPEG images

entities.label: Christ Church Library MS 152
entities.description: A fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript used by Furnivall in two represented runs after Hengwrt breaks at X.1180: ff. 262r-271v and ff. 274r-276v. Missing ff. 272-273 are supplied internally from Add MS 5140 at [887]-[944], and Add MS 5140 supplies the final passage after [1044].

physical_description.extent: The Canterbury Tales on ff. 2-276v; Gamelyn on ff. 58v-71v
notes: The official description calls the Tales basically complete but disordered, records missing ff. 272-273, and records the terminal break in the Parson's Tale after line 1044.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian complete 684-canvas IIIF presentation of Christ Church MS 152
entities.description: The complete provider-published digital object. Furnivall's two represented Parson runs are ff. 262r-271v at canvases 523-542 and ff. 274r-276v at canvases 543-548; the ranges are adjacent in the digital sequence because lost ff. 272-273 are absent.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with JPEG images

entities.label: British Library Add MS 5140
entities.description: A late-fifteenth-century Canterbury Tales manuscript supplying two discontiguous Parson's Tale passages in Furnivall: [887]-[944] from ff. 348r-351r inside the Christ Church continuation, then the final passage after [1044] and Retraction from ff. 355v-357v. It also supplies the appendix Nun's Priest-Second Nun link.

physical_description.extent: The Canterbury Tales ff. 2r-359v; Parson's Tale ff. 315r-359v

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 1758
entities.description: The principal appendix source for Gamelyn after its lost opening. Furnivall also uses f. 192v for the separately bracketed single-line Monk-Nun's Priest link addition at VII.3967, absent from Hengwrt.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 17 D XV
entities.description: The source for the first thirteen lines of Furnivall's appendix Gamelyn text before Harley MS 1758 begins.

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 18 C II
entities.description: A Canterbury Tales witness used for Furnivall's appendix Man of Law-Shipman link; for the six-line Monk-Nun's Priest link addition VII.3961-3966 from f. 226r; and at ff. 165r and 172v for two short Canon's Yeoman supplements.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: second quarter of the fifteenth century

physical_description.extent: The Canterbury Tales ff. 3r-271r; Gamelyn ff. 56v-67v

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 19 C XI
entities.description: An early-fifteenth-century French moral miscellany containing a Tale of Melibee on ff. 52r-65v. Furnivall cites a brief French comparison passage at f. 58r, column 2, around paragraph [2525]-[2526]; this is supporting source-language material, not the English base text.

physical_description.extent: i + 156 folios; French Tale of Melibee ff. 52r-65v

entities.label: British Library Royal MS 19 C VII
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century Northern French manuscript containing Renaut de Louhans's French Melibee and Prudence on ff. 123r-148v. Furnivall cites a brief French comparison phrase at f. 142v around paragraph [2854]; this is supporting source-language material, not the English base text.

physical_description.extent: ii + 167 folios; French Melibee and Prudence ff. 123r-148v

entities.label: British Library complete 347-canvas IIIF presentation of Royal MS 19 C VII
entities.description: The complete provider-published digital object. The French comparison phrase cited by Furnivall is on f. 142v, canvas 292 of 347.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 3 manifest with JPEG images

entities.label: British Library Harley MS 7333
entities.description: The source for Furnivall's appendix Clerk-Merchant link.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: second half of the fifteenth century

entities.label: Lichfield Cathedral Canterbury Tales manuscript (current MS 29; Furnivall's MS 2)
entities.description: The principal source for Furnivall's appendix Second Nun-Canon's Yeoman link, preamble, and Canon's Yeoman Tale, represented from ff. 172v-183v apart from two short Royal MS 18 C II supplements. The source edition and BL rotograph catalog use historical shelfmark MS 2; current scholarship reports MS 29, but the exact renumbering authority remains to be reconciled.

entities.label: British Library rotograph of Lichfield Cathedral MS 2 (MS Facsimile 405/47)
entities.description: A 1940 rotograph cataloged by the British Library under historical source designation Lichfield Cathedral MS 2 and siglum Lc. Its exact folio coverage was not established.

entities.label: Huntington Library MS EL 26 C 9 (Ellesmere Chaucer)
entities.description: The manuscript source from which the edition's twenty-three teller portraits were copied and cut on wood by William Harcourt Hooper.

physical_description.extent: 232 main text folios plus preliminary and final leaves

entities.label: Huntington 480-canvas published IIIF presentation of the Ellesmere Chaucer
entities.description: The complete official 480-canvas published sequence, including all 232 main folios and eight published preliminary or final leaves. The physical description lists twelve non-text leaves, so the provider sequence omits four cataloged non-text leaves.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest with JPEG images

subject.description: Principal Canterbury Tales sequence through the surviving Hengwrt text

object.description: The extant Canterbury Tales on the original parchment leaves

scope.notes: Hengwrt lacks G.554-1481 and X.1180-end. Furnivall's supplements for those gaps are separate relations.

subject.description: Four bracketed additions in the Wife of Bath's Preamble

object.description: The corresponding four discontiguous Dd.4.24 passages

scope.notes: These are four discrete supplies, not one continuous Dd.4.24 run; canvas 116/f. 74v lies between target leaves but contains no target addition.

subject.description: The Christ Church portions of the represented Parson's Tale continuation after the Hengwrt loss
locators: represented through [1044], excluding the internal Add MS 5140 supply [887]-[944]

object.description: Two discontiguous represented runs of the Parson's Tale in Christ Church MS 152

scope.notes: Christ Church has lost ff. 272-273. Furnivall therefore changes to Add MS 5140 ff. 348r-351r for [887]-[944], returns to Christ Church at f. 274r through f. 276v, then changes to Add MS 5140 again after [1044].

subject.description: Six-line Monk-Nun's Priest link addition absent from Hengwrt

object.description: Royal MS 18 C II source passage

scope.notes: Furnivall prints VII.3961-3966 as one bracketed Royal MS 18 C II supply, followed by the separately bracketed Harley MS 1758 line VII.3967.

subject.description: Separately bracketed single-line Monk-Nun's Priest link addition absent from Hengwrt

object.description: Harley MS 1758 source line

scope.notes: This single bracketed line follows the separate Royal MS 18 C II supply at VII.3961-3966.

subject.description: Bracketed French comparison passage embedded in the Tale of Melibee
locators: appartiennent aucune foiz a orgeul ... Aprez len fait les tours et les edifices

object.description: French Melibee comparison text

scope.notes: This is a source-language quotation or comparison relation. Royal MS 19 C XI is not modeled as the transmission base for Furnivall's Middle English Melibee text.

subject.description: Bracketed French comparison phrase embedded in the Tale of Melibee
locators: ne plus quil scet se il est dines de lamour de dieu

object.description: French Melibee comparison text

scope.notes: This is a source-language quotation or comparison relation. Royal MS 19 C VII is not modeled as the transmission base for Furnivall's Middle English Melibee text.

subject.description: Two discontiguous Parson's Tale supplies and Chaucer's Retraction
locators: [887]-[944], inserted inside the Christ Church continuation
after [1044] through the final passage and Retraction

object.description: The two corresponding Add MS 5140 passages
locators: ff. 355v-357v after [1044] through the ending and Retraction

scope.notes: The edition returns from Add MS 5140 to Christ Church MS 152 after [944]; Add MS 5140 is not merely a single terminal source.

subject.description: The first thirteen lines of the appendix Tale of Gamelyn

subject.description: The Lichfield-derived portions of the Second Nun-Canon's Yeoman link, Canon's Yeoman preamble, and Canon's Yeoman Tale
locators: represented Canon sequence excluding Royal MS 18 C II supplements G.717 and G.1337-1340

object.description: Canon's Yeoman sequence in the Lichfield manuscript

scope.notes: Furnivall and the BL rotograph catalog call the witness Lichfield Cathedral MS 2; current reporting calls it MS 29. Royal MS 18 C II supplies the isolated line G.717 and the four-line span G.1337-1340 inside this otherwise Lichfield-derived sequence.

subject.description: Two bracketed supplements inside the appendix Canon's Yeoman sequence
locators: CT G.717: But natheles yet wol I telle you part
CT G.1337-1340: Vnto the goldsmyth with these teynes three ... But they were as hem ought to be

object.description: Corresponding Royal MS 18 C II readings

scope.notes: The XML bracket closes after G.1340 although its punctuation is typographically awkward; the defensible encoded scope is G.1337-1340.

subject.description: Twenty-three teller portrait plates copied and cut on wood by William Harcourt Hooper
locators: twenty-three plates following appendix matter in the checked copy

object.description: The corresponding twenty-three Ellesmere teller portraits

scope.notes: This is a visual-source relationship, not a claim that the edition's text transcribes Ellesmere.

subject.description: Complete 518-canvas provider-published digital object

object.description: Covers, binding matter, manuscript leaves, edges, and 3D view as represented by NLW

scope.notes: The corpus work occupies original parchment ff. 2r-250v, IIIF canvases 15-512.

subject.description: Complete 377-canvas provider-published digital object

object.description: MS Dd.4.24 as represented by the complete provider-published presentation

scope.notes: The four work additions occupy f. 73v/canvas 114, f. 74r/canvas 115, and f. 75r/canvas 117; canvas 116/f. 74v is not a target locus. The CUDL description states that 36 conservation-inserted replacement leaves are not digitized except for two leaves incorporating manuscript remnants, so complete means the entire provider-published presentation rather than every physical insertion.

subject.description: Complete 684-canvas provider-published digital object

object.description: Christ Church MS 152 as represented by the complete provider-published presentation

scope.notes: The work portions are ff. 262r-271v/canvases 523-542 and ff. 274r-276v/canvases 543-548. These ranges are adjacent because lost ff. 272-273 are absent from the physical manuscript and provider sequence; Furnivall inserts Add MS 5140 [887]-[944] between the two Christ Church textual runs.

subject.description: Complete 347-canvas provider-published digital object

object.description: Royal MS 19 C VII as represented by the complete provider-published presentation

scope.notes: The agz8233 supporting quotation occupies f. 142v/canvas 292; the complete cache retains all 347 provider canvases.

subject.description: The one-item rotograph held as MS Facsimile 405/47

object.description: Unknown portion of the source manuscript cataloged historically as Lichfield Cathedral MS 2

subject.description: Complete 480-canvas provider-published digital object

object.description: The Ellesmere manuscript as represented by the complete provider-published presentation

scope.notes: The cached published sequence is complete relative to its 480-canvas inventory, but the physical extent lists four additional non-text leaves not reproduced by the provider. This work uses only the twenty-three discontiguous portrait loci, recorded in the edition-to-witness relation and access work_portion.

local_copies.label: British Library complete Royal MS 19 C VII IIIF bundle
notes: Royal MS 19 C VII does not contain the target Canterbury Tales edition or its Middle English Melibee text. Furnivall cites a French source-analogue phrase from f. 142v/canvas 292; that supporting relationship and exact image locator are recorded in the scoped cites relation, not as a target work portion.
Exact target-support image URL: https://bl.digirati.io/images/ark:/81055/vdc_100165761662.0x00011c/full/full/0/default.jpg
ZIP integrity, exact member set, all 347 inventory hashes and byte counts, JPEG signatures, and Pillow decodes were verified.

The requested 1600-pixel profile resolved through the provider's IIIF v3 service to inventory URLs using the full size path and approximately 2001-pixel-high derivatives; the exact returned URLs and dimensions remain auditable in inventory.json.

rights.component: Medieval Canterbury Tales and Gamelyn textual content represented in the XML
rights.basis: The underlying medieval works were created and transmitted centuries before modern copyright.
notes: This does not determine rights in nineteenth-century editorial additions, XML markup, or modern images.

rights.component: XML serialization, OCR, structural markup, and metadata
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The repository root Unlicense is phrased for software, while the XML header and current U-M item carry separate access and rights language. No checked source expressly resolves the Unlicense's scope for this data artifact.

rights.component: Materials presented in current U-M item AGZ8233.0001.001
rights.basis: U-M currently labels CME materials public domain in the United States and supplies a No Copyright-United States statement.
notes: The XML header's educational and research access language and the repository's software-worded Unlicense are retained as separate scope questions.

rights.component: Historical page-image presentation linked from the current U-M item
rights.basis: U-M applies its current No Copyright-United States statement to the item materials, which reproduce a nineteenth-century edition.
notes: The exact direct image service terms and a downloadable complete bundle were not verified.

rights.component: Published nineteenth-century text, editorial arrangement, and Hooper's wood engravings
rights.basis: The six parts were published in 1869-1881, well before 1931; Furnivall died in 1910 and Hooper in 1912.

rights.component: Digitized scan of the nineteenth-century edition
rights.basis: Wikimedia Commons identifies the scan as public domain in the United States because the represented publication predates 1931.

rights.component: Medieval textual and artistic content of the manuscript
rights.basis: The manuscript was produced around 1395-1405.
notes: Physical access restrictions and rights in modern reproductions are separate.

rights.component: NLW IIIF manifest and digital images
rights.basis: The item-specific IIIF manifest carries the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0. NLW states that it does not claim rights in reproductions of public-domain works, while attribution and separate high-resolution request routes remain provider terms.
notes: NLW's Public Domain Mark guidance says marked works may be used, including commercially; acknowledge the National Library of Wales and verify any requested higher-resolution service terms separately.

rights.component: Medieval manuscript text
rights.basis: The manuscript is dated to the fifteenth century.
notes: The downloaded modern catalog description is metadata-only and is not treated as public-domain manuscript content.

rights.basis: Ownership and custody of a physical manuscript are not themselves copyright statuses; the repository restricts physical circulation and provides separate surrogate and reproduction routes.

rights.component: Exact Hengwrt Canterbury Tales readings demonstrably published by Furnivall in 1869-1881
rights.basis: Furnivall published these exact witness readings in the nineteenth-century source edition, and the relevant authorial and editorial terms have expired.
notes: This conclusion is limited to the exact readings made available in Furnivall's edition, not every unprinted variant, annotation, or physical feature of the manuscript.

rights.component: Any manuscript variants, annotations, art, or other content not shown to have been validly made available to the public before the end of 1988
rights.basis: UK IPO guidance says some literary works unpublished at the end of 1988 may remain protected through 31 December 2039 even when their authors died centuries ago; exact publication history for this residual content was not established.

rights.component: Exact Christ Church Parson's Tale readings at ff. 262r-271v and 274r-276v demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Furnivall published these exact witness passages in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant authorial and editorial terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the exact passages demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: Modern three-page Christ Church MS 152 catalog-description PDF, including its prose, selection, and layout
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The official PDF was downloaded for metadata evidence, but no item-specific open license or public-domain statement for its modern catalog text and layout was verified.
notes: Metadata-only local caching does not grant permission to republish the PDF.

rights.component: Exact readings Furnivall published from ff. 348r-351r at [887]-[944], ff. 355v-357v after [1044] through the Retraction, and f. 282v for the appendix Nun's Priest link
rights.basis: Furnivall published these exact witness passages in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant authorial and editorial terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the exact passages demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: Exact Gamelyn readings after line 13 through line 902 from ff. 46-55 and the Monk-Nun's Priest link line VII.3967 from f. 192v, demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Furnivall published these exact witness readings in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the exact Gamelyn passage and VII.3967 demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: The first thirteen Gamelyn lines demonstrably published by Furnivall from ff. 66v-67r
rights.basis: Furnivall published these exact witness readings in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the first thirteen Gamelyn lines demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: The Man of Law-Shipman link from f. 82r, Monk-Nun's Priest link VII.3961-3966 from f. 226r, Canon line G.717 from f. 165r, and Canon lines G.1337-1340 from f. 172v, demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Furnivall published this exact witness passage in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the exact Man of Law-Shipman, Monk-Nun's Priest, and Canon passages demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: The Clerk-Merchant link demonstrably published by Furnivall from f. 68v, column 2
rights.basis: Furnivall published this exact witness passage in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the Clerk-Merchant link demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: The Second Nun-Canon's Yeoman link, preamble, and Canon's Yeoman Tale demonstrably published by Furnivall from ff. 172v-183v, excluding Royal MS 18 C II supplements G.717 and G.1337-1340
rights.basis: Furnivall published this exact witness sequence in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the Canon's Yeoman sequence demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: The twenty-three teller portrait designs at ff. 10r, 34r, 41v, 46v, 49r, 63r, 76v, 80v, 87v, 102v, 115r, 123v, 133r, 136r, 143v, 148v, 151v, 168r, 179r, 187r, 192r, 202r, and 206r as demonstrably published in Hooper's wood engravings
rights.basis: Furnivall's nineteenth-century edition published Hooper's copies of these exact portrait designs, and Hooper died in 1912; the relevant terms have expired.
notes: This does not govern modern manuscript photographs or details not demonstrably made available through the nineteenth-century engravings.

rights.component: Any manuscript text, art, annotations, or visual details outside the twenty-three portrait designs demonstrably published by Furnivall and Hooper
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: Medieval textual content of MS Dd.4.24
rights.basis: The underlying medieval text was created and transmitted centuries before modern copyright.
notes: This conclusion does not govern CUDL photographs, metadata, or access terms.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as held by Cambridge University Library
rights.jurisdiction: Copyright status of physical custody and ownership
rights.basis: Ownership and custody of the physical codex are separate from copyright in its contents or digital reproductions.

rights.component: Exact Wife-Preamble passages III.575-584, 609-612, 619-626, and 717-720 demonstrably published by Furnivall from ff. 73v, 74r, and 75r
rights.basis: Furnivall published these exact witness passages in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the four exact Wife-Preamble passages demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: Downloadable CUDL page images in the complete MS Dd.4.24 IIIF bundle
rights.basis: The exact CUDL manifest licenses downloadable images under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0; this file license is distinct from rights in the published medieval passages.
notes: The noncommercial restriction does not authorize for-profit print-on-demand reuse without separate permission.

rights.component: CUDL IIIF manifest and descriptive metadata for MS Dd.4.24
rights.basis: The exact CUDL manifest separately licenses metadata under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0.

rights.component: Christ Church MS 152 page images in the Digital Bodleian presentation and cached JPEG bundle
rights.jurisdiction: Provider statement and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: The item-specific manifest states that the photographs are copyright The Governing Body of Christ Church, Oxford, all rights reserved, and that permission to reproduce images must be obtained from the Christ Church College Librarian.
notes: Public IIIF access and local research caching do not authorize repository redistribution or print-on-demand use of the images.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian IIIF manifest structure and descriptive metadata for Christ Church MS 152
rights.jurisdiction: Provider terms and intended place of reuse
rights.basis: No open metadata license was present in the exact manifest. The manifest's explicit all-rights-reserved statement addresses the photographs, so this record does not silently extend it to every metadata field or infer an open metadata license.

rights.component: Medieval French textual content, including the comparison passage cited by Furnivall at f. 58r, column 2
rights.basis: The underlying medieval text was created and transmitted centuries before modern copyright.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as held by the British Library
rights.jurisdiction: Copyright status of physical custody and ownership
rights.basis: Ownership and custody of the physical codex are separate from copyright in its contents or digital reproductions.

rights.basis: Furnivall published this exact witness passage in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant terms have expired.

rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: Medieval French textual content, including the comparison phrase cited by Furnivall at f. 142v
rights.basis: The underlying medieval text was created and transmitted centuries before modern copyright.

rights.component: Physical manuscript as held by the British Library
rights.jurisdiction: Copyright status of physical custody and ownership
rights.basis: Ownership and custody of the physical codex are separate from copyright in its contents or digital reproductions.

rights.component: Exact French comparison phrase at f. 142v around Furnivall paragraph [2854]
rights.basis: Furnivall published this exact witness passage in the nineteenth-century edition, and the relevant terms have expired.

rights.component: Any manuscript readings, variants, annotations, art, or other content outside the exact French comparison phrase demonstrably published by Furnivall
rights.basis: Exact valid-publication history was not established for residual manuscript content; UK protection through 31 December 2039 may apply to works still unpublished at the end of 1988.

rights.component: British Library IIIF manifest and digital images for Royal MS 19 C VII
rights.basis: The item-specific IIIF Presentation manifest carries the Creative Commons Public Domain Mark 1.0 and labels the usage terms Public Domain.
notes: The mark is item-specific. Citation, physical-access conditions, and rights in unrelated British Library resources remain separate.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Printed edition matter and page sequence
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR at TEI Libraries Level 1 with omitted matter represented by milestones and 804 PB links
editorial_practices.description: U-M encodes the edition's principal text and appendix matter as an uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 text, marks omitted matter, links historical page images, and serializes appendix matter after main p. 654 rather than reproducing the insertion point of Gamelyn in the checked bound copy.
notes: The record does not infer unadvertised OCR correction or normalization.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Incomplete Hengwrt Canterbury Tales plus identified passages, source quotations, and images from eleven additional witnesses
editorial_practices.representation: Principal Hengwrt-order transcription with passage-level supplements and a separately labeled appendix
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall follows Hengwrt where it survives, inserts four Dd.4.24 Wife-Preamble passages and a Royal/Harley Monk-Nun's Priest link, retains two French Royal 19 Melibee source quotations, changes to Christ Church MS 152 and Add MS 5140 for the lost ending, and separately prints Gamelyn, three alternative links, the principally Lichfield Canon's Yeoman sequence with Royal supplements, and twenty-three Ellesmere-derived teller portraits.
editorial_practices.scope: Whole six-part edition, with passage scopes represented by source relations
notes: The compilation boundary must not be flattened into a claim that every printed passage derives from Hengwrt.

evidence.citation: General Corpus / CME, agz8233 source XML at pinned commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.locator: HEADER; FRONT; BODY; BACK; PB, MILESTONE, and manuscript-source labels
evidence.summary: Exact repository artifact. The header identifies U-M item AGZ8233.0001.001, the 2006 uncorrected OCR/TEI Level 1 encoding, 804 linked page images, Furnivall's edition, title-page and issue dates, and the edition's source. A complete non-generic MILESTONE audit confirms Dd.4.24 Wife-Preamble supplies, distinct Royal 18 C II and Harley 1758 Monk-Nun link supplies, two Royal 19 French source quotations, the composite Christ Church/Add MS 5140 Parson route, and Royal 18 C II/Lichfield Canon source transitions, in addition to the separately modeled appendix witnesses.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME repository LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The repository-level Unlicense text refers to software. It does not expressly resolve the status of third-party textual data, U-M terms, nineteenth-century editorial content, or manuscript images.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item AGZ8233.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Official item presentation confirming title, source edition, full-text public access, linked historical page views, and current No Copyright-United States item treatment.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, About the Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse.
evidence.summary: Describes collection history, TEI encoding and educational or research purposes, and the current public-domain/No Copyright-United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: Access check of the historical page-image route linked from U-M item AGZ8233.0001.001.
evidence.summary: The item presents page-image links and the repository XML contains 804 unique sequential PB references. A tested direct page-image request returned HTTP 403, and no stable complete-download route was verified during this review.

evidence.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Hengwrt MS of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, ed. Frederick J. Furnivall (London: Chaucer Society, 1868-1879), complete bound-copy scan.
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 9-793; title, contents, main pp. 1-654, appendix pp. 1*-57*, binding note, and twenty-three plates
evidence.summary: Complete 800-page scan verified locally. It identifies Furnivall, the Chaucer Society, publication details, the Hengwrt transcription, transitions to Christ Church MS 152 and Add MS 5140, every appendix witness and folio locator, the Lichfield Canon's Yeoman sequence, and Hooper's twenty-three Ellesmere-derived plates. The bound copy inserts Gamelyn after main p. 128, unlike the U-M encoding's all-appendices-after-p.654 serialization.
notes: Local qpdf integrity check passed; the PDF is unencrypted and has 800 pages.

evidence.citation: Wikimedia Commons file page for The Hengwrt ms of Chaucer's Canterbury tales, Internet Archive scan.
evidence.summary: Provides the direct full-PDF route, Internet Archive provenance, file metadata, and the provider's United States public-domain assertion.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library, catalog record 51491, The Hengwrt ms of Chaucer's Canterbury tales.
evidence.summary: Confirms editor, series, six-part issue dates 1869-1881, bibliographic extent x, [3], 654 [i.e. 659], 30*, [27] pages, two leaves and twenty-three plates, and notes that announced Cambridge Gg.4.27 figures may never have been issued. The Folger copy itself contains only part 2.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales Archives and Manuscripts, Peniarth MS 392D, Hengwrt Chaucer.
evidence.summary: Official repository description of Peniarth MS 392D, former Hengwrt MS 154, [1395]-[1405] production in London, 258-folio structure with original parchment ff. 2-250, incomplete Canterbury Tales, restricted-original access, and availability of a digital version.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, Peniarth MS 392D, official IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.summary: Complete 518-canvas provider-published object covering covers, boards, old-binding leaf, manuscript leaves, edges, and 3D view. The manifest carries Public Domain Mark 1.0 and NLW attribution. Full local bundle audit verified every inventory hash, byte count, canvas ID, label, image URL, and image-member set.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, Public Domain Mark.
evidence.summary: Explains NLW's use of the Public Domain Mark and that marked works may be used, including commercially, while acknowledgment is requested.

evidence.citation: National Library of Wales, Intellectual Property Rights Policy.
evidence.summary: Separates item-specific rights marks and provider services, says NLW does not claim rights in faithful reproductions of public-domain works, and provides enquiry routes for higher-resolution services and restricted uses.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, IIIF Presentation 2 manifest for MS Dd.4.24.
evidence.summary: Official complete provider presentation supporting the shelfmark, first-quarter fifteenth-century date, extent, precise target folios and canvases, and separate CUDL licenses: downloadable images CC BY-NC 4.0 and metadata CC BY-NC-ND 4.0. The description notes that most conservation-inserted replacement leaves were not digitized.

evidence.citation: Deep inventory and image-integrity audit of the complete local CUDL MS Dd.4.24 1600-pixel IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: All 377 inventory records and images; target canvases 114, 115, and 117
evidence.summary: Verifies ZIP integrity, every inventory-recorded image checksum and byte count, complete 377-canvas provider coverage, and target labels 73v, 74r, and 75r. Canvas 116/74v is not a target locus.

evidence.citation: Christ Church Library, MS 152 official catalog description.
evidence.summary: Official three-page description dating MS 152 to the fifteenth century, identifying The Canterbury Tales on ff. 2-276v and Gamelyn on ff. 58v-71v, and describing the Tales as basically complete but disordered with a Parson's Tale break. The cached PDF is metadata-only and contains no target manuscript facsimile.

evidence.citation: Digital Bodleian, Christ Church MS 152, complete IIIF Presentation manifest and locally audited image bundle.
evidence.locator: 684 canvases; ff. 262r-271v at c523-542 and ff. 274r-276v at c543-548
evidence.summary: Official complete provider presentation of MS 152. The inventory maps both Parson runs exactly; lost ff. 272-273 are absent, making c523-548 adjacent. Deep audit verified ZIP integrity, the exact manifest/inventory/image member set, all 684 image hashes and byte counts, JPEG signatures, and Pillow decodes. The manifest states Photo copyright The Governing Body of Christ Church, Oxford, all rights reserved, with permission required from the Christ Church College Librarian for reproduction.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Add MS 5140.
evidence.summary: Official catalog description dating the manuscript c. 1485-1500 and identifying The Canterbury Tales on ff. 2r-359v and the Parson's Tale on ff. 315r-359v. Images were unavailable when checked.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 1758.
evidence.summary: Official catalog description dating the manuscript to the mid-fifteenth century and locating Gamelyn on ff. 46-55. Furnivall's source labels show that its opening is lost and Royal MS 17 D XV supplies the first thirteen printed lines.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 17 D XV.
evidence.summary: Official catalog description dating the manuscript to 1450-1499 and identifying Gamelyn on ff. 66v-79v. Images were unavailable when checked.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 18 C II.
evidence.summary: Official catalog description dating the manuscript to the second quarter of the fifteenth century and identifying The Canterbury Tales on ff. 3r-271r. Images were unavailable when checked.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 19 C XI.
evidence.locator: Tale of Melibee ff. 52r-65v; target f. 58r, column 2
evidence.summary: Official catalog description of an early-fifteenth-century French moral miscellany, including the French Tale of Melibee beginning at f. 52r. The record supports shelfmark, date, language, physical extent, access conditions, and the context of Furnivall's f. 58r column 2 comparison quotation; no public complete facsimile is linked.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Royal MS 19 C VII.
evidence.locator: Renaut de Louhans, Melibee and Prudence, ff. 123r-148v; target f. 142v
evidence.summary: Official catalog description of the mid-fifteenth-century Northern French parchment manuscript, its French Melibee and Prudence locus, physical extent, access conditions, and complete digitized-content link.

evidence.citation: British Library, Royal MS 19 C VII, complete IIIF Presentation 3 manifest and locally audited image bundle.
evidence.locator: 347 canvases; supporting French quotation at f. 142v/canvas 292
evidence.summary: Complete official provider presentation. Deep audit verified ZIP integrity, the exact manifest/inventory/image member set, all 347 inventory byte counts and SHA-256 values, JPEG signatures, and Pillow decodes. The item manifest carries Public Domain Mark 1.0 and labels usage terms Public Domain. Royal 19 C VII does not contain the target Canterbury Tales text; f. 142v supplies only a French source-analogue quotation cited by Furnivall.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 7333.
evidence.summary: Official catalog description dating the manuscript to the second half of the fifteenth century and identifying The Canterbury Tales on ff. 37r-118r. Furnivall locates the appendix Clerk-Merchant link at f. 68v, column 2.

evidence.citation: Lichfield Cathedral, The Library.
evidence.summary: Official institutional page describing the cathedral library and enquiry or private-visit route. No complete public digital facsimile or item-level online record for the Canterbury Tales manuscript was verified.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, MS Facsimile 405/47, rotograph of Lichfield Cathedral MS 2.
evidence.summary: Official catalog record for a 1940 one-item rotograph, naming source Lichfield Cathedral MS 2 and siglum Lc. The exact photographed folio coverage and its reconciliation with reported current shelfmark MS 29 were not established.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, mssEL 26 C 9, Ellesmere manuscript of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales.
evidence.summary: Official item evidence for shelfmark, c. 1400-1410 date, parchment support, 232 main text folios, manuscript identity, and physical-access restriction.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Ellesmere Chaucer, official IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.summary: Complete official 480-canvas published inventory reproducing all 232 main text folios and eight preliminary or final leaves. The physical extent lists twelve non-text leaves, so four cataloged non-text leaves are absent. The locally reused verified bundle contains identical provider manifest and 480 JPEGs, with an exact ContentDM source inventory.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Library, Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: States that Huntington does not require permission or fees to quote or publish public-domain material or material whose copyright it does not own, requests citation, and places rights determination responsibility on the researcher.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Digitale Bibliothek, Frederick James Furnivall, GND 117540579.
evidence.summary: Authority evidence for Furnivall's identity, birth on 4 February 1825, death on 2 July 1910, and identifiers GND 117540579, VIAF 71467450, and LCNAF n79032923.

evidence.citation: British Museum, William Harcourt Hooper, BIOG32010.
evidence.summary: Official authority evidence identifying Hooper as a wood engraver, 1834-1912. The source edition credits him with cutting the twenty-three Ellesmere teller portraits on wood.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright.
evidence.summary: Authoritative general term guidance used to separate medieval and nineteenth-century public-domain content from modern reproductions, data markup, and provider terms.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of Copyright (term guidance).
evidence.summary: Official United Kingdom term guidance supporting the distinction between exact nineteenth-century-published passages and residual manuscript content whose valid publication history is unestablished, including transitional protection that may continue through 31 December 2039 for certain works unpublished at the end of 1988.

open_questions.question: Is there a stable complete-download or IIIF route for all 804 U-M historical page images, and why did the tested direct image request return HTTP 403?
next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Collections for a supported bulk-image or IIIF endpoint for AGZ8233.0001.001.
If supplied, cache the complete provider object and map the edition portion without replacing the current Commons scan route.
Do not infer permanent unavailability from one automated 403 response.

open_questions.question: Were the announced figures from Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.27 ever issued with any state of Furnivall's six-part edition?
next_steps: Compare complete institutional copies and wrappers for all six parts.
Consult Chaucer Society issue records and correspondence.
Keep the twenty-three Ellesmere plates distinct from the possibly unissued Gg.4.27 figures.

open_questions.question: Which appendix and plate order belongs to each original part issue, and which differences in surviving bound copies reflect later binder choices?
next_steps: Inspect original wrappers or unbound sets of First Series nos. 3, 9, 27, 39, 51, and 71.
Compare additional complete institutional copies with the checked Commons scan.
Retain carrier order and editorial or intellectual order as separate fields.

open_questions.question: What authoritative renumbering links Furnivall's Lichfield Cathedral MS 2 to reported current MS 29, and what portion does BL rotograph MS Facsimile 405/47 reproduce?
next_steps: Ask Lichfield Cathedral Library for the current shelfmark, historical shelfmark concordance, and digital-reproduction options.
Ask the British Library for the rotograph's frame or folio inventory and reproduction terms.
Preserve both historical MS 2 and reported current MS 29 until an institutional concordance is obtained.

open_questions.question: Are complete lawful digital facsimiles or reproduction-order services available for the remaining uncached transmission witnesses Add MS 5140, Harley MS 1758, Royal MS 17 D XV, Royal MS 18 C II, Harley MS 7333, and the Lichfield manuscript?
next_steps: Contact the British Library and Lichfield Cathedral Library with the exact shelfmarks and passage folios recorded here.
Request whole-manuscript digital reproductions where practical, not cropped target passages alone.
If complete files are supplied, cache each under source-cache/agz8233 and record exact target-work portions and reuse terms.

open_questions.question: Does the CME repository's software-worded Unlicense govern agz8233 XML data and markup, and how does it interact with U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement and item access language?
next_steps: Ask the CME maintainers to state the intended license for source/CME_phase_1-2/agz8233.xml and its markup.
Ask U-M for written clarification before redistributing a close copy of the encoding in a commercial workflow.
Keep medieval text, nineteenth-century edition, XML serialization, and modern images as separate rights layers.

open_questions.question: What item-specific redistribution terms govern Huntington's Ellesmere images, the modern Christ Church catalog PDF, the British Library Lichfield rotograph, and any future manuscript images obtained by request?
next_steps: Request written commercial and repository-redistribution guidance from each provider before publishing modern image files.
Treat NLW and Royal MS 19 C VII item-specific Public Domain Marks separately from unmarked provider resources and from Christ Church MS 152 images that explicitly require permission.
Record permissions, fees, required credits, and territorial limitations on the exact access object.

open_questions.question: Which individuals besides Furnivall performed the manuscript transcription, checking, proofing, and production work for each of the six parts?
next_steps: Consult original part wrappers, Chaucer Society reports, and Furnivall correspondence.
Do not assign every production act to Furnivall solely from the editor credit.
Add scoped contributor agents only when direct evidence identifies them.
